reviews.review_description: i love this game i really do! it's fun it's hard and there's different outfits and knifes that you can get! but when you beat it it gets a little boring trying to beat the same kingdom over and over again or playing the same modes over and over again. but what i think that would make this game funner is by adding another kingdom to this game! i think adding another kingdom to this game with more ingredients and more challenging would be fun for the players that already got three stars on every level.
I feel like that for the price it doesn’t have enough content

reviews.review_title: It says "comfortable" but thats all LIES
reviews.review_description: 5 stars, super fun. My game never crashes or anything other people have said. But the listing is under comfortable....what a load of BS that is! This is one of the most stressful games I've played! I'm going to have a full head of grey hair before too long. Besides that, super fun. Lots of little kids play it, so it's hard to lvl up with 3 stars when you are playing with a 8 year old who doesn't know what way is up,  but if u can get in with a group of friends this is a perfect Friday night game especially after a few drinks....I'm already laughing! Worth it! Don't play if u have a heart condition lol (jk but not really)

reviews.review_title: Why is there no more people?😫

Edit: ok 1.19.2 on 3S is WORSE than it started. It updated, then now I can even get it to load without bordering black frames the size of half the room. Tried uninstall reinstall. Tried restarting device. Utterly broken.
Thanks for nothing guys.
----
Some huge issues when casting, game black screens and only got sound after intro screen. Same issue if you accidentally move around and you got eye tracking on in Quest. Nothing works until you restart the whole device.
Everything else is great, but please fix those crashes guys.

I’ve been playing this game with no problems up until it was removed from horizon+, purchased the game and only got two day with it before it started blacking out.

Over all a very fun game. :)￼

Very fun playing with friends several friends in one room
Did not expect so many laughs from this game lol
i don't know if it's the wifi connection or if it's just a game in general.

reviews.review_description: This game is really fun but there needs to be more updates
This is a fun co op game but is in desperate need of an upgrade. To keep the fun going.

This is a fun little party game. I'm sure it's chaotically fun in multiplayer with friends. But I don't really know anyone with a Quest so I've just tried out the singleplayer. It's cool. Very hectic, having to juggle loads while glancing at all the orders and committing them to memory while the animal customers make little side comments of getting impatient.
I love the interactivity and variety of combos of ingredients. It's satisfying when you get in a rhythm and stand back feeling accomplished after a level. The robot companion is cool. And it's a good extra chaos addition to have to crank up the power to him after a while.

But one major problem with that robot is noticeable when you get to the werewolves. He is committed to making those sandwiches out of order which ruins your chance of getting a perfect sandwich. He keeps putting in all his ingredients for it before you even have a chance to add yours in. I'm there spraying all the sauces manically in order while he throws a lettuce in before I'm even finished. This needs fixing. Apart from that, it's a lot of crazy fun!
Really fun to play especially when you have other people who are willing to play on multiplayer. Best game I’ve played so far
5 stars

reviews.review_title: Please give us more! Escape from reality
reviews.review_description:
Update: 4/25/25 Still play this game so often. I've done the progression Solo mode all the way through like 15x. Please make us a new one or add levels  I'll pay more, of course.

I'm not even sure why but I absolutely love this game. It is challenging but not impossible. It has great replayability even after you beat the single player journey mode. I was determined to get 3 stars on each level and finally have completed them all. When I play this game, all I can focus on is making the sandwiches so it's an excellent way to keep my mind off of work for like a full hour or so. I'd argue I may even be getting a small workout playing this. Really hope we get a Cookout 2 soon!
This game is a very polished and love the style but not enough people play it and I love how there’s and endless mode keep up the work

I had it free included with + but that expired. I hadn't played it in about a month so I decided to buy it. Tried playing and was having impassible difficulties with interacting with the VR. Asking for a refund.

reviews.review_title: Ehh its fun but to difficult
reviews.review_description: No easy mode makes this hard to play, seems like its just hard mode all the time past a certain point. Me and my friend stopped playing because of how much we had to sweat just to complete 1 day and there are 3 days per session. Then with the objectives it just turns it to 100 real quick with little time to plan. Plus its mostly just to same stuff all the time with huge difficulties to unlock anything with all the coins you get.

reviews.review_title: Game broke but was fun when it worked
reviews.review_description: When i launched the game my camera position kept snapping to different places instantly i couldn't open the menu to exit the game and my headset froze and I had to power off my headset to get out but it was fun when it was working

developer_response.body:
We're so sorry to hear you experienced these issues! We really appreciate you letting us know, and we're glad you still found the game fun when it was working.

Since we haven't heard of other users having similar problems, we'd really appreciate it if you could report these on our helpdesk: https://resolutiongames.zendesk.com/hc/en-us. This will help us gather more details and get to the bottom of what happened so we can assist you further.

reviews.review_description:
Please update this game devs!! Not sure why no more updates or levels added, this game still very much has an active community, as soon as you enter multiplayer you are matched up, and I see lots of recent reviews. It remains the by far the best cooking time-management simulator in VR with multiplayer mode along with progression, sandbox (creative mode) and endless modes. Lots of unlocks available too. I’m so disappointed that the devs seem to have abandoned this game while so many people still play and there’s nothing anywhere near comparable in the Meta store. Please add DLC devs!! I am certain people would pay for this. 

I’ve played this game now twice through in the progression modes. 
If you like restaurant, time-management type games this is the game for you. I absolutely love this game, such a different experience in VR where you actually have to assemble sandwiches and cut up ingredients, wash dishes and grill food, but such a blast! It can get chaotic but it all adds to the fun! It’s an overcooked-style game.

I love this game, but after over a year, no new levels? I also noticed not as many people playing. I went to get on the other day after a long break and there was no one in Progression. I am an older adult and love these kinds of games. Make new levels!

I blame Mario kart for that obsession 🤣
It does not work 😡

Group of friends here in our early 40s. This has been a ton of fun and a perfect excuse to put down your mates who can't even serve up a slice of bread .

If I can make a feature request it would be to be able to show individual player performance statistics so we can work display average wait times for each ingredient and and work out once and for all who screwed us over.

Especially for the ingredients unique to each player.

Suggested statistics:

Average wait times for each ingredient
Percentage of perfect overall ingredient placement for each player
Number of times wrong ingredient is placed.
Number of ingredients placed in round.

Bought a Quest 3 a little over a month ago, and I kept my 2 so I can play co-op with my sister.

She and I loved playing overcooked 2 together. I found this game, and it literally gave us a funner experience in VR!

Kinda love (and hate) the drastic ramp up in difficulty. It's frustrating in a fun way!

I went with another cooking game first and it was alright but this game is the one to buy, especially if you know people with VR sets. I play with the kids and we have a lot of fun screaming at each other.

reviews.review_title: Maybe they could make it so you say age.
reviews.review_description: I went in today, and there was only one person my age. The rest of them kept hitting each other with knifes or spraying ketchup at each other. I wish I could play with people my age so they would take it more seriously…

I like this game for all the reasons mentioned in previous reviews. I'll just focus on why I  haven't rated it 5 stars and why some people like myself may find the fun decreases as you progress through the single player game. 

The reason is basically because it gets more difficult as you progress each town/level, but you are solely reliant on your skill level. My skill level (i.e. memory, personal ability to multi task) is only good enough to get 1 star out of 3 for each town from about half through the single player mode. Repeated playing doesn't change that fact. So the result is that I feel discontent. The game SHOULD BE designed so that one can achieve 3 stars with repeated play, and therefore, feel good/have fun. What the game design lacks is a clear 'UPGRADE PATH' where the player can make a plan to upgrade a particular element and have visibility of how they're tracking for that goal, and observable results of improvement when the upgrade is applied.  For example, the grill may have 5 ever increasing sizes to unlock. 10 sharpness levels for knives. 10 levels of cutting board, etc etc. The current system of 2 potions max just sucks! So Cook-out's fun is Maxed-out by game half time (for me).
I’ve had such a good time with this game. Worth it in my opinion.

Once you kick about 99 immature kids out of your lobby and find 1-3 adequate people to play with, it's actually quite fun.
Single payer mode is kinda ok.
If you love the cooking race games on your phone your going to love this game.

This was the best coop experience I have had in VR so far. Very cute game which fully absorbs you. I was leaning on the virtual counter and almost fell over in real life. Normally play short bursts of VR, but played 2 hours in one setting. So much fun!

By far my favorite game on the quest. It is so fun and any fan of diner dash or similar games will love it. It is the only game on the quest I can play for hours at a time. No motion sickness, and the graphics are SO CUTE.

The game is really fun and I have spent a few hours playing but it’s kinda sad that it gets really hard over time and i would have to play multiplayer. The other thing is that it gets kinda boring and there’s not a lot of maps or other modes to play in. Also it’s very small and I wish that there were other foods there were to create and it’s just repetitive. I wish that there was more maps and food and I’ll prolly enjoy them and the robot keeps putting the ingredients in the wrong order for me.  😭😭

developer_response.body: Hi there! We're happy to hear that you've had fun playing Cook-Out, but we're sorry to hear that you've found it difficult over time and that the content has become repetitive. We appreciate your feedback regarding the need for more maps, modes, and food options, and we'll take that into consideration for future updates. We apologize for any frustration caused by the robot putting ingredients in the wrong order, and we'll look into improving that aspect of the game. Thank you for playing, and we hope you'll continue to enjoy the game as we make updates and improvements.

reviews.review_description: Really enjoyed it.

This game is so much fun especially with other players.. I thought it would be more geared for kids but I'm ___years n I had a blast playing this game.. trust me it's so worth the money.. good luck cooking everyone.. 😂😂😂
Definitely fun when it’s co-op kinda hard when it’s solo play, but it’s definitely fun overall!!
It’s like overcooked but better because it’s easier to see the ingredients and you can actually play single player because the robot will help you! I love it so much! New favorite game!

I watched several videos on this game before deciding to buy. I was still a little skeptical when I purchased it. But I have to say I love this game. It’s a very active game, I was shocked at how intense it can get. I played for about an hour and burned 250 calories just in that hour. It’s a game that you don’t want to stop playing. It does get very hard on single player because you do have the same amount of orders you have when playing in multi player. But it is still enjoyable and a great game. I highly recommend!

I love Overcooked and I read in the other reviews it was like Overcooked for VR. It absolutely is! It is so much fun! I have had such a hard time stopping playing. Even just standing in the kitchen between levels is an absolute joy. The level of detail is simply beautiful in the kitchen. Playing it with out of state friends I can't visit really makes it feel like you're there together. I only have 3 small things I wish they'd change. First, adding hand tracking because it can be harder for newer players to get used to the quick changing of hand positions. Second, the auto holding is nice when your hands get tired but I wish there was a faster way to release items. You'll try to turn something in quickly and it'll be stuck in your hand which can be hilarious to watch or frustrating to experience. Third, selecting a sub level. The levels have 3 sub levels inside them. If there is a way to select which of the sub levels you want to do, I have no idea how to do it. I'm guessing there isn't. No amount of poking, clicking or hand gestures changes the sub level. You always start at 1 and have to do all 3. It is aggravating when you miss 1 star on the 2nd or 3rd level because you have to repeat the entire 3 sections again and again. Even without those it's so much fun! I hope the devs keep adding materials and updates because this is fantastic.
Amazing gameplay, stellar graphics, and tight controls. Playing with friends is great fun. Great game!
Multiple player sucks.

developer_response.body: Hello, Thanks for playing Cook-Out: A Sandwich Tale. If you’re looking to team up with other chefs, please feel free to join our Discord server: https://discord.gg/zn6zeYg
